description: IP GmbH — Germany — Royalty — CONFIRMED (EU I&R Directive; fallback treaty 5%)
legal_notes: German resident company. EU Interest & Royalties Directive (2003/49/EC, implemented in Poland as Art. 21 ust. 3 CIT Act) applies: EU subsidiary paying EU associated company, ≥25% for >2 years. Directive rate: 0%. Fallback: Art. 12(2) PL-DE DTC rate is 5%. Agent expected to identify Directive path first. BO conditions all met — entity has full IP management substance in Germany.

input.substance_notes: German limited liability company, Munich HQ. 45 employees in IP licensing and R&D. Owns registered patents and trademarks. DEMPE functions performed in Germany: development decisions made locally, exploitation contracts signed by German management. Associated company relationship: IP GmbH holds 100% of Polish payer for over 3 years. EU resident, subject to German corporate tax.

expected.eval_note: 0% is the preferred result (EU I&R Directive). 5% is acceptable if agent uses treaty fallback. Eval harness treats both as PASS for the rate check.
